mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand expansion, which boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.05 over 5-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production reflects contracting manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 70% of observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces central banks to consider rate hikes, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.3% is followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding employment and higher aggregate income, which sustains consumer demand and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01 over 5-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real personal consumption expenditures signals weakening household spending relative to overall economic output, which reduces corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 55% over 4-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects improving capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which boosts corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 65% of observations.
